ddls:
CREATE TABLE "hd_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter (e.g., data_confidence_level) */,
  "parameter_value" REAL /* Value of the parameter */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter's purpose */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the parameter */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the parameter (e.g., integer, float, string) */,
  "validation_rules" TEXT /* Validation rules for the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data quality entry */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parameter related to data quality */,
  "rejection_threshold" REAL /* Threshold for rejecting low-quality data */,
  "quality_metric" TEXT /* Quality metric used for data validation */,
  "validation_rules" TEXT /* Validation rules for the quality threshold */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES hd_parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_windows" (
  "window_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis window configuration */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the related parameter */,
  "increment" REAL /* Increment value for the sliding window in seconds */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the analysis window properties */,
  "window_type" TEXT /* Type of the analysis window (e.g., sliding, fixed) */,
  "validation_rules" TEXT /* Validation rules for the window configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("window_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_windows_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES hd_parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment entry */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parameter indicating segment information */,
  "num_segments" INTEGER /* Number of segments to collect with lowest HR */,
  "segment_type" TEXT /* Type of the segment (e.g., time-based, event-based) */,
  "validation_rules" TEXT /* Validation rules for the segment configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_segments_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES hd_parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, clinician) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the parameter */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the parameter was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES hd_parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
